content.text: Now let me check the Widget component to understand the full structure:
Let me check the App.tsx to see how Planning page is rendered:

Perfect! Now I have a complete understanding of the Planning page structure. Let me create a comprehensive analysis document. ## Analysis: Planning Page Structure & Scrolling Issue Based on my investigation, here's the complete structure and the scrolling problem: ### Current Structure (Top-to-Bottom) ``` App.tsx ↓ Layout.tsx (main container with sidebar/terminal margins) ↓
element with px-6 padding ↓ Planning.tsx (Page Component) └─
├─ Page Header Section (mb-6) │ ├─

"Planning & Calendar" │ └─

Description └─ Suspense fallback └─ Calendar.tsx (Lazy loaded Widget) └─ Widget component ├─ Widget Header (flex-shrink-0, DOESN'T SCROLL) │ └─ "Calendar" title + draggable controls └─ Widget Content (flex-1 overflow-auto) └─

├─ Calendar Navigation (calendar-nav) │ ├─ Prev button │ ├─ Month display │ ├─ View mode buttons (Month/Week/Day/List) │ └─ Next button └─ Calendar Grid (flex-1 min-h-0) └─ MonthView / WeekView / DayView / AgendaView ``` ### The Problem Currently, the entire Calendar widget content (including the navigation bar with Prev/Month/View buttons/Next) scrolls together. Here's what's happening: 1. **Widget.tsx** (line 56): `
` - This allows the ENTIRE Widget content to scroll 2. **Calendar.tsx** (lines 333-476): The calendar-widget is a flex column with navigation (calendar-nav) and grid in it 3. **Result**: When you scroll, BOTH the calendar navigation AND the calendar view content scroll together ### What Needs to Stay Frozen According to your requirements: 1. Planning page header ("Planning & Calendar" title + description) - already frozen (at page level) 2. Widget header ("Calendar" title) - already frozen (flex-shrink-0) 3. Calendar Navigation bar (Prev, Month display, View mode buttons, Next) - **CURRENTLY SCROLLING, NEEDS TO FREEZE** ### What Should Scroll Only the calendar view content: - MonthView (day cells) - WeekView (time slots) - DayView (hours) - AgendaView (event list) ### Root Cause The `Widget.tsx` component wraps the entire Calendar in `overflow-auto`, which scrolls everything. The Calendar component doesn't have internal scrolling—it just all scrolls together. ### Files That Need Changes 1. **src/components/Widget.tsx** - Modify to support "sticky header" mode or pass control to child component 2. **src/widgets/Calendar.tsx** - Restructure to have: - Non-scrolling navigation section - Scrollable content section with only the calendar views 3. **src/pages/Planning.tsx** - Might need height constraints to enable scrolling (currently might be `min-h-0` missing) ### Recommended Approach Two options: **Option A: Calendar manages its own scrolling (Recommended)** - Remove the `overflow-auto` from Widget content container - Make Calendar internally manage scrolling with a scrollable `.calendar-content` div - Keep `.calendar-nav` outside the scrollable area - Widget Header + Calendar Nav stay frozen, Calendar content scrolls **Option B: Widget supports sticky headers** - Add a property to Widget component to make header "sticky" - Would be more generic but more complex **Option A is better** because: - Cleaner separation of concerns (Calendar handles its own layout) - The Calendar already has a `calendar-widget` wrapper with flex column structure - We just need to split that structure to have scrollable/non-scrollable parts ### Code Changes Summary 1. **Widget.tsx**: Remove padding from content div or make it conditional 2. **Calendar.tsx**: - Modify line 334: `
` stays - Keep navigation (lines 336-402) unscrolled - Wrap calendar grid (lines 405-475) in a `
` 3. **Planning.tsx**: Ensure page container allows Calendar to expand properly Let me present this analysis and ask for your confirmation before proceeding.
